title: Delivery driver car accident - investigation required

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: Horizon Mutual claims, this is Alana. Are you calling to report a workplace injury?

turns.speaker: Caller
turns.utterance: Yes. I'm a delivery driver and got into a minor crash while on route yesterday. My neck is stiff and painful.

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: I'm sorry to hear that. Were you operating your employer's vehicle during a scheduled delivery?

turns.speaker: Caller
turns.utterance: Yes, company van, route stop 12. It happened around 5:15 PM.

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: Were police called, and was an accident report filed?

turns.speaker: Caller
turns.utterance: Yes, police came. I have a report number, but I don't have it in front of me.

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: No problem. Did you seek treatment?

turns.speaker: Caller
turns.utterance: I went to the ER last night. They said cervical strain and told me to follow up in three days.

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: Thank you. Because this involves a motor vehicle incident, we'll coordinate workers' comp and auto liability review. I'll open the claim, but status will be under investigation.

turns.speaker: Caller
turns.utterance: Does that mean treatment is delayed?

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: Emergency and initial necessary care is typically not delayed. Ongoing treatment authorization depends on investigation findings.

turns.speaker: Agent
turns.utterance: Your claim number is WC-612990. Please send the police report number and ER discharge paperwork when available.

turns.speaker: Caller
turns.utterance: I can send those by tomorrow morning.
